page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-4 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The memory configuration includes 16.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB per core. The CPU cache architecture consists of a 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 25%. However, multi-core and memory-bound operations show limitations. Multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng, Geekbench multi-core tests, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only and write large-block tests registering in the bottom 25%. For specialized tasks, LLM inference on a small 135M model achieves average speeds, but performance drops to the bottom 10% when processing a medium 7B model.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-standard-4 offers a standard resource density suitable for entry-level general-purpose workloads that do not demand high memory bandwidth or intensive parallel processing. Given its strong single-threaded execution but weaker multi-core scaling, database operations, and web serving throughput, this instance is best suited for lightweight single-threaded applications, basic text processing, small-scale software utilities, and low-concurrency development environments.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-4 is an x86_64 general-purpose server instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It features 2 physical cores, 2 threads per core, and a 32 MB L3 cache, with no local storage or GPU acceleration. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU execution and text processing, both ranking in the top 25%. However, the instance exhibits weaker multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth, with stress-ng multi-core and large-block memory read/write speeds falling into the bottom 25%. Qualitative cost efficiency is aligned with standard resource density for entry-level workloads. This instance is suited for single-threaded applications, lightweight text processing, and low-concurrency development tasks rather than parallelized databases or high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-4 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 4 vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but exhibits weaker multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities.
